title: A People's History of the United States: 1492 - Present
rationale: Book #1 for the economic-and-financial-history subcategory (empty leaf, Phase 0). A People's History is the definitive work of American 'history from below' and a landmark in revisionist economic historiography — it carries the category's core concerns about class conflict, labor exploitation, elite economic power, imperial expansion, and the relationship between material conditions and historical change. First published 1980; this entry records the current Harper Perennial Modern Classics edition (2015).
